title: CI/CD Pipelines
summary: Pipeline design, GitHub Actions, deployment strategies (blue-green, canary), GitOps with ArgoCD.

sections.content:
## CI/CD Fundamentals

- **Continuous Integration (CI)** — every code push triggers automated build + tests. Catch bugs early, keep main branch deployable.
- **Continuous Delivery (CD)** — every green build produces a deployable artifact. Deployment to production is a manual trigger.
- **Continuous Deployment** — every green build is automatically deployed to production. No manual gate.

The goal: **reduce the cost and risk of deployment** so you can deploy frequently and confidently.

sections.title: CI/CD Pipeline Stages
sections.content:
graph LR
    Commit[Git Push] --> Build[Build\nmvn package]
    Build --> Test[Unit Tests\nmvn test]
    Test --> Analyze[Static Analysis\nSonarQube]
    Analyze --> IntTest[Integration Tests\nTestcontainers]
    IntTest --> Docker[Build Docker Image]
    Docker --> Scan[Security Scan\nTrivy]
    Scan --> Push[Push to Registry]
    Push --> DeployStaging[Deploy to Staging]
    DeployStaging --> E2E[E2E Tests]
    E2E -->|manual gate| DeployProd[Deploy to Production]
    style Commit fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style DeployProd fill:#059669,color:#fff

## GitHub Actions — Spring Boot Pipeline

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Set up JDK 21
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '21'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven
      
      - name: Build and Test
        run: mvn verify
      
      - name: Upload test results
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: target/surefire-reports/

  build-image:
    needs: build-and-test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Build Docker image
        run: |
          docker build -t $IMAGE_NAME:$GITHUB_SHA .
          docker tag $IMAGE_NAME:$GITHUB_SHA $IMAGE_NAME:latest
      
      - name: Trivy vulnerability scan
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          image-ref: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:${{ github.sha }}
          exit-code: '1'
          severity: 'CRITICAL'
      
      - name: Push to registry
        run: docker push $IMAGE_NAME:$GITHUB_SHA
```

## Deployment Strategies

### Blue-Green Deployment
Run two identical production environments. Switch traffic from Blue (current) to Green (new) atomically.

```
Blue (v1) ← traffic    Green (v2) idle
          [deploy + test Green]
Blue (v1) idle          Green (v2) ← traffic
          [keep Blue for quick rollback]
```

✓ Zero downtime, instant rollback  
✗ 2x infrastructure cost during transition

### Canary Deployment
Gradually shift traffic to new version:

```
5% → v2 (watch metrics for 10 min)
20% → v2 (watch metrics for 20 min)
50% → v2 (watch metrics for 30 min)
100% → v2 (decommission v1)
```

✓ Low blast radius, data-driven rollout  
✗ Must handle multiple versions simultaneously

sections.title: Canary Deployment Traffic Shift
sections.content:
graph LR
    LB[Load Balancer] -->|95%| V1[Service v1\n10 pods]
    LB -->|5%| V2[Service v2\n1 pod canary]
    Monitor[Prometheus/Grafana\nWatch error rate, latency]
    V2 --> Monitor
    Monitor -->|metrics OK| Scale[Scale canary to 50%]
    Monitor -->|metrics BAD| Rollback[Rollback: 0% to v2]
    style V2 fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style Rollback fill:#dc2626,color:#fff

## GitOps with ArgoCD

GitOps treats Git as the single source of truth for infrastructure and application state. ArgoCD continuously reconciles the cluster state with what's in Git.

```yaml
# ArgoCD Application manifest
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: order-service
spec:
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/org/k8s-manifests
    targetRevision: HEAD
    path: services/order-service/production
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: production
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true      # delete resources removed from git
      selfHeal: true   # revert manual cluster changes
```

**Flow:**
1. Developer merges PR → updates `image.tag` in k8s-manifests repo
2. ArgoCD detects Git change
3. ArgoCD applies new manifests to cluster
4. ArgoCD reports sync status back to Git/Slack

sections.question: What is the core principle of GitOps?
options: All deployments must be done via Git pull requests
Git is the single source of truth — desired state is declared in Git and a controller continuously reconciles the actual state to match
GitOps means using Git for both application code and infrastructure code in the same repository
All team members must review and approve changes before deployment
sections.explanation: GitOps declares the desired system state in Git. An agent (like ArgoCD) watches Git and continuously reconciles the live cluster to match. This means: deployments happen by committing to Git, manual cluster changes are automatically reverted, and the full deployment history is in Git's commit log.
